title: Churn rate by contract
prompt:
Using the preloaded churn DataFrame **`df`**, compare churn between contract types. Define:

- `monthly_rate` — churn rate of `contract == 'monthly'` customers, rounded to 4 decimals
- `annual_rate` — churn rate of `contract == 'annual'` customers, rounded to 4 decimals
- `rate_diff` — `monthly_rate - annual_rate`, rounded to 4 decimals

(Churn rate = mean of the 0/1 `churned` column.)
starter_code:
# df is preloaded

monthly_rate = ...
annual_rate = ...
rate_diff = ...
reference_solution:
monthly_rate = round(float(df.loc[df.contract == 'monthly', 'churned'].mean()), 4)
annual_rate = round(float(df.loc[df.contract == 'annual', 'churned'].mean()), 4)
rate_diff = round(monthly_rate - annual_rate, 4)
hint_notes: groupby('contract')['churned'].mean() is the idiom. Tags: groupby-idiom, rate-vs-count-confusion.
